entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #1 — Launching WEAPONIZED — A New Consensus Reality
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp introduce WEAPONIZED and describe a changing public reality in which military personnel, scientists, journalists, and citizens can discuss UFO/UAP evidence more openly.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #10 — Duncan Trussell — UFOs, AI, Spirituality, and Reality
entities.summary: Duncan Trussell joins Corbell and Knapp for a wide-ranging discussion about UFOs, artificial intelligence, spirituality, Buddhism, psychedelics, gurus, and the nature of reality.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #100 — Creatures From Another World? The UFO–Cryptid Connection
entities.summary: Author and filmmaker David Paulides discusses reported links among UFOs, Sasquatch, disappearances, portals, Skinwalker Ranch, and evidence presented in American Sasquatch: Man, Myth, or Monster.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #101 — Congressional UAP Champions on What Comes Next
entities.summary: Representatives Anna Paulina Luna, Tim Burchett, and Eric Burlison discuss resistance to congressional oversight, blocked witness access, intimidation, the limits of public hearings, possible presidential action, and alternative strategies.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #102 — Jacques Vallée — The Bizarre Nature of UFOs
entities.summary: Jacques Vallée joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp for a wide-ranging discussion of UFO history, Project Blue Book, remote viewing, NIDS, AAWSAP, crash retrievals, the Trinity case, congressional secrecy, science, and paths forward.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #103 — 2025 Year in Review — Progress, Evidence, and Pushback
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review WEAPONIZED’s 2025 reporting, including new witnesses, released UAP imagery, congressional engagement, major books and films, mystery-drone incursions, and the organized pushback against UFO transparency.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #104 — Nazca Mummies — Scientific Criticism and Evidence Tampering
entities.summary: Will Brown, biological anthropologist Dan Proctor, and radiologist Michele Adams examine claims surrounding three-fingered South American mummies, alleged specimen manipulation, black-market trafficking, and the evidence needed to establish authenticity.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #105 — James Fox — New Varginha Witnesses and the Continuing Cover-Up
entities.summary: Filmmaker James Fox discusses new witnesses connected to the 1996 Varginha incident, alleged intimidation, efforts to locate medical personnel and video evidence, and a Washington press event intended to bring firsthand testimony before the public and Congress.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #106 — Ryan “Neo” Bodenheimer — Four Pilot UAP Encounters
entities.summary: Retired Air Force lieutenant colonel and former Thunderbird Ryan Bodenheimer describes four aerial UAP encounters, including a close call, and explains why the observed objects did not resemble conventional aerodynamic craft.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #107 — Soviet UFO Programs — SETKA, Thread 3, and Nuclear Incursions
entities.summary: George Knapp revisits his 1990s reporting from Russia and releases documents describing Soviet UFO investigations, including the SETKA collection program, Thread 3 technology analysis, pilot and satellite cases, and an alleged 1982 intrusion at a Ukrainian nuclear missile base.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #108 — Syria UAP Video — Apparent Instantaneous Acceleration
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell, George Knapp, and Marik von Rennenkampff analyze newly released intelligence footage from the Jordanian-Syrian border showing an object tracked by an MQ-9 Reaper and appearing to demonstrate extraordinary acceleration.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #109 — Bob Lazar in Austin, Syria UAP Debate, and Area 51 Access
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp review Bob Lazar’s surprise Austin appearance, responses to criticism of the Syria UAP footage, an unauthorized Papoose Lake intrusion, and Representative Eric Burlison’s stated plans to visit restricted facilities including Area 51.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #11 — John Long — Hollywood, UFO Projects, and the Art of the Movie Trailer
entities.summary: John Long, co-founder of Buddha Jones, joins Corbell and Knapp to discuss changes reshaping Hollywood, the future of theatrical and streaming entertainment, UFO-themed productions including NOPE, and the creative craft of movie-trailer marketing.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #110 — Chris Sharp — Former AARO Deputy Director Describes Unexplained Cases
entities.summary: Journalist Chris Sharp previews his interview with former AARO deputy director Tim Phillips, including claims that dozens of cases showed extraordinary capabilities, some UAP were detected in space, and classified videos remain unreleased.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #111 — Lenval Logan — UAP Task Force Analysis and Better Classified Evidence
entities.summary: Former Air Force intelligence analyst and UAP Task Force member Lenval Logan assesses the Syria UAP footage, describes classified evidence stronger than public releases, discusses AWACS tracking of anomalous objects, and explains why original task-force personnel were excluded from AARO.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #112 — Eric Burlison — Classified Site Visit and Congressional UAP Oversight
entities.summary: Representative Eric Burlison discusses a classified facility visit, limits on what he can reveal, the credibility of whistleblowers, congressional efforts to obtain concealed UAP records, and his evolving view after reviewing sensitive information firsthand.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #115 — A New Chapter in the Bob Lazar Saga
entities.summary: Luigi Venditelli joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp to discuss S4: The Bob Lazar Story, its reconstruction of the alleged S4 facility, and the continuing debate surrounding Bob Lazar’s claims.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #12 — Colm Kelleher Part Two — AAWSAP, the Hitchhiker Effect, and Crash Retrievals
entities.summary: In the second half of the Colm Kelleher interview, the former AAWSAP project manager discusses anomalous effects associated with Skinwalker Ranch, the program’s technology objectives, and his stated knowledge of UFO crash-retrieval efforts.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #13 — The Russian UFO Files — Military and Scientific Investigations
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp examine George Knapp’s post-Soviet reporting on Russian military and scientific UFO investigations, including interviews with officials and researchers connected to a decade-long nationwide Ministry of Defense program.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #14 — AARO Testifies to the Senate — Sean Kirkpatrick and the Middle East UAP
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp review Sean Kirkpatrick’s public testimony before a Senate subcommittee chaired by Kirsten Gillibrand, including AARO’s evidence claims and newly presented Middle East UAP footage.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #15 — Dave Foley — Comedy, UFO Curiosity, and a Southern California Encounter
entities.summary: Actor and comedian Dave Foley discusses his career, his longstanding interest in UFOs, the Storm Area 51 event, conversations with Joe Rogan, and an unexpected UFO encounter in Southern California.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #16 — Secret Soviet UFO Science — Researchers Behind the Iron Curtain
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp revisit covert and semi-covert UFO research conducted by Soviet scientists and academic groups, drawing on interviews George Knapp recorded during reporting trips to Russia in the 1990s.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #17 — Robbie Williams — Music, Unusual Experiences, and Skinwalker Ranch
entities.summary: Robbie Williams discusses his career, personal challenges, media scrutiny, unusual experiences, a visit to Skinwalker Ranch, and future creative projects.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #18 — The Mojave Triangle UAP — Marine Witnesses at Twentynine Palms
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp present videos, photographs, and witness accounts from Marines who reported a large triangular object over the Twentynine Palms training base in April 2021.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #19 — The Mojave Triangle Investigation Continues — New Marine Testimony
entities.summary: Following the initial Twentynine Palms release, Corbell and Knapp provide additional Marine interviews, respond to flare explanations, and explain why they consider the Mojave Triangle incident an open investigation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #2 — John “Guts” Gutierrez and the 2019 Navy UAP Swarms
entities.summary: Navy commander John “Guts” Gutierrez discusses his aviation background and the 2019 West Coast incidents in which multiple Navy warships reported sustained incursions by numerous unidentified objects.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #20 — Ben Schneider and Lord Huron — Music, Myth, Ghosts, and Cosmic Questions
entities.summary: Ben Schneider of Lord Huron discusses the band’s exploration of death, time, folklore, ghosts, cosmology, and lost love, along with the musical influences and creative environment behind its work.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #21 — The David Grusch Revelations
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp recount their early contact with David Grusch, his decision to come forward publicly, his whistleblower complaint, and reported retaliation connected to his UAP disclosures.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #22 — Allied UAP Reverse-Engineering Programs and the Larry Maguire Letter
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine Canadian MP Larry Maguire’s letter alleging that Five Eyes nations have coordinated programs to analyze and reverse-engineer recovered UAP materials, including material reportedly held by Defence Research and Development Canada.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #23 — Whitley Strieber — Communion, The Visitors, and Readiness for Disclosure
entities.summary: Author Whitley Strieber discusses his decades of reported encounters with non-human intelligences, the cultural impact of Communion, evolving ideas about the Visitors, crash-retrieval allegations, whistleblowers, and whether society is prepared for disclosure.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #24 — Congressional Momentum, Canadian Transparency, and Barriers to UAP Disclosure
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review expanding U.S. and Canadian legislative efforts to uncover classified UAP records, alleged recovered hardware, special-access programs, and contractor-held materials while assessing institutional barriers to disclosure.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #25 — Tim Burchett, Congressional UFO Hearings, and the Coming House Testimony
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review the history of congressional UFO hearings and interview Representative Tim Burchett about the anticipated July 2023 House hearing, prospective witnesses, bipartisan interest, and alleged hidden crash-retrieval materials.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #26 — The UAP Disclosure Act, Chuck Schumer, and the Upcoming House Hearing
entities.summary: British journalist Chris Sharp joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp to examine Senator Chuck Schumer’s proposed UAP disclosure legislation, its focus on government and contractor-held records and materials, Harry Reid’s influence, and the approaching House hearing.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #27 — The Historic House UAP Hearing and a Fresh Look at the Nimitz Tic Tac Case
entities.summary: Ahead of the July 2023 House hearing, Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp explain why the testimony of David Grusch, David Fravor, and Ryan Graves should be considered historic and revisit the Nimitz Tic Tac encounter through a key eyewitness account.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #28 — Congressional Hearing Roundtable
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp are joined by Ross Coulthart and Bryce Zabel for a roundtable about the July 26, 2023 House UAP hearing, its three witnesses, how the hearing came together, and what might follow.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #29 — David Fravor and the Nimitz Tic Tac Encounter
entities.summary: WEAPONIZED revisits the 2004 Nimitz Carrier Strike Group encounters, Commander David Fravor’s pursuit of the Tic Tac object, the delayed official investigation, and why his testimony became central to modern demands for UAP transparency.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #3 — Jay Stratton — AAWSAP, AATIP, and the UAP Task Force
entities.summary: Former intelligence officer Jay Stratton discusses his roles across AAWSAP, AATIP, and the UAP Task Force, including early work on the Tic Tac case and classified briefings delivered across government.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #30 — David Grusch, Whistleblower Retaliation, and the Attempt to Discredit His Testimony
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine efforts to discredit David Grusch after his public testimony, including reporting about his prior PTSD treatment, and discuss retaliation, whistleblower protections, and the potential chilling effect on other witnesses.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #31 — Wayne Pacelle — Factory Farming, Animal Welfare, and Corporate Power
entities.summary: Animal-welfare advocate Wayne Pacelle discusses suffering in industrial agriculture, the effects of factory-farming business models on animals and people, state-level reforms, and efforts by large agricultural interests to override those protections.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #4 — Spy Balloons, Unknown Objects, and UAP Transparency
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp examine the Chinese surveillance balloon and three additional objects shot down over North America, with Dave Foley joining a discussion about transparency, confusion, and links to the 2019 UAP swarms.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #42 — The Sol Foundation Conference and Grusch Revelations
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp are joined by citizen journalist Joe Murgia to discuss the inaugural Sol Foundation symposium, David Grusch’s recent public comments, congressional pressure surrounding the UAP Disclosure Act, and the Syria “Dome” UAP photograph.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #43 — The Unreleased Philip Corso Tapes
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp revisit Philip J. Corso’s Roswell technology-transfer claims and examine previously unreleased interviews recorded by personnel associated with the National Institute for Discovery Science.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #44 — The Fight Over the UAP Disclosure Act
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine the weakening of proposed UAP disclosure legislation and speak with Liberation Times editor and publisher Chris Sharp about congressional opposition and defense-contractor influence.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #45 — John Alexander on Crash Retrievals, NIDS, and the Sol Conference
entities.summary: John B. Alexander joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp to discuss the Advanced Theoretical Physics program, alleged crash-retrieval programs, NIDS, Skinwalker Ranch, the Sol Foundation conference, and Philip J. Corso.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #46 — Richard Dolan on Disclosure, History, and USO Cases
entities.summary: Historian Richard Dolan joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp for a wide-ranging discussion of disclosure, intelligence and defense institutions, whistleblower revelations, congressional statements, academic interest, global UFO history, and unidentified submerged object cases.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #47 — The Jellyfish UAP Investigation
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp explain how they obtained and evaluated thermal video of the alleged Jellyfish UAP recorded at a U.S. military base in Iraq, discuss reported transmedium behavior, and respond to proposed conventional explanations.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #48 — Ernest Cline, UFO History, and Science-Fiction Culture
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp visit author and screenwriter Ernest Cline to discuss his career, UFO research interests, science-fiction influences, and extensive collection of pop-culture and UFO materials.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #49 — Pushback, AARO, and the UFO Information War
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine institutional and media pushback against UAP disclosure efforts, discuss former AARO director Sean Kirkpatrick, and include a segment featuring Bob Lazar.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #5 — Colm Kelleher — AAWSAP and Scientific Anomaly Research
entities.summary: Biochemist and AAWSAP program manager Colm Kelleher discusses his scientific background, NIDS anomaly investigations, government-funded UAP research, and the study of unusual physical and biological effects.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #50 — Washington Update on UAP Transparency and Congressional Oversight
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp report from Washington, D.C. on the status of congressional UAP oversight, possible future hearings, and concerns shared in meetings with lawmakers, staff, and firsthand witnesses.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #51 — Unreleased Episode Placeholder
entities.summary: The official WEAPONIZED catalog reserves Episode #51 but does not publish episode content, stating only that some episodes are worth the wait.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #52 — Season Finale and AARO Historical Report Review
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review major moments from the first season, revisit interviews with government investigators, journalists, and disclosure advocates, and criticize AARO’s newly released historical report on U.S. government UFO investigations.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #53 — Kona Blue, AARO, and Continuing UAP Incursions
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review the proposed Kona Blue program, dispute public characterizations of whistleblower testimony, and discuss continuing drone and UAP incursions over sensitive U.S. military airspace.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #54 — Pantex, Nuclear-Site UAP Incursions, and FOIA Records
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine reported UAP incursions around nuclear facilities and are joined by Dustin Slaughter to discuss redacted records concerning a 2015 diamond-shaped craft near the Pantex plant.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #55 — AARO, Congressional Oversight, and the Kirkpatrick Interview
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp mark the first anniversary of the July 2023 House UAP hearing and speak with Marik von Rennenkampff about his interview with former AARO director Sean Kirkpatrick, congressional disclosure efforts, and disputed assessments of major UAP cases.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #56 — Las Vegas Update, Dave Navarro, and Crop Circle History
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell reconnects with George Knapp in Las Vegas, discusses projects underway behind the scenes, notes musician Dave Navarro’s return to the stage and UFO interest, and revisits a 1994 UK crop-circle investigation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #57 — The Colares UFO Attacks and Brazilian Military Files
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp speak with Brazilian investigator Thiago Ticchetti about the Colares UFO incidents, the Brazilian Air Force investigation, alleged injuries, and claims that high-quality military imagery was transferred to the United States.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #58 — UFO Transparency in the Corridors of Power
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp discuss the collapse of proposed 2024 UAP disclosure amendments, plans for additional congressional hearings, and Lester Nare’s Capitol Hill advocacy for continued UAP transparency.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #59 — Journalists vs. Secrecy
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp speak with journalists Chris Sharp and Matt Laslo about professional attacks on UAP reporters, Sharp’s reporting on an alleged multi-agency ocean operation, and the status of disclosure legislation and future hearings.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #6 — The Defense Executive, a Recovered Craft, and a Living Occupant
entities.summary: George Knapp and Jeremy Corbell revisit a former defense-industry executive’s account involving a recovered craft from New Mexico and an allegedly living occupant, including a promised recorded deposition.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #60 — WEAPONIZED Series Overview
entities.summary: The official Episode #60 page contains a general description of the WEAPONIZED investigative series rather than a topic-specific episode synopsis. This record preserves the publication date and official source without inventing guests, cases, or claims.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #61 — The Tic Tac at Twenty
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp mark the twentieth anniversary of the 2004 Nimitz Tic Tac encounter with David Fravor, reviewing the carrier group’s sensor detections, Fravor’s visual encounter, and the case’s role in renewed government and public attention to UAP.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #62 — Sky Intruders, Drones, and a Transmedium UFO Video
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp speak with Chris Sharp about recurring incursions near UK military facilities and with underwater archaeologist Rory Kremer about video of an object appearing to descend into the Pacific Ocean, while also reviewing the November 2024 congressional UAP hearing.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #63 — Mystery Drones and Australia’s Saucer Nests
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine unresolved mystery-drone incursions in New Jersey and near UK military bases, then speak with Australian journalists Danielle O’Neal and Piia Wirsu about their investigation of Queensland “saucer nests” and UFO history.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #64 — The 2024 UAP Year in Review
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp join citizen journalists Joe Murgia, Ryan Robbins, and Danny Silva to review major UAP developments, controversies, disappointments, and expectations for 2025.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #65 — UFO Revolution Premiere Q&A
entities.summary: At the Ojai Playhouse premiere of UFO Revolution, Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp discuss the documentary’s account of UAP transparency efforts, whistleblowers, Congress, and disputed handling of the Immaculate Constellation report.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #66 — Mike Gold on NASA, UAP, and Space Research
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp speak with space executive and NASA adviser Mike Gold about NASA archives, advanced-material research in zero gravity, historical UAP data, and public-private cooperation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #67 — Richard Dolan’s History of USOs
entities.summary: Historian Richard Dolan discusses documented reports of unidentified submerged objects, alleged transmedium craft, maritime witnesses, and cases presented in A History of USOs, Volume 1.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #68 — Ralph Blumenthal and the 2017 New York Times UFO Story
entities.summary: Ralph Blumenthal describes how he, Helene Cooper, and Leslie Kean reported the landmark 2017 New York Times UFO story, the editorial resistance they encountered, and his research into psychiatrist John Mack.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #69 — John Mack, Ariel School, and Reporting the Unreportable
entities.summary: In the second part of the Ralph Blumenthal interview, the discussion focuses on John Mack’s abduction research, Harvard’s review of his work, the Ariel School encounter, and barriers facing modern UAP whistleblowers.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #7 — Storm Area 51 and the Baghdad Phantom
entities.summary: The hosts examine the cultural impact and legal aftermath of the Storm Area 51 phenomenon and release multiple images of an object recorded by a U.S. Air Force Reaper drone and described as a phantom.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #70 — The Age of Disclosure and Media Coverage of UAP
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp discuss The Age of Disclosure, media treatment of UAP witnesses, and omissions they identify in coverage of mystery-drone and 2019 Navy ship incidents.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #71 — The Vandenberg Red Square UAP
entities.summary: Air Force veteran Jeff Nuccetelli describes reported UAP incursions at Vandenberg, including a large glowing red square or rectangular object, and discusses a released military police blotter tied to the event.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #72 — The USS Jackson Tic Tac Video
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp unveil video reportedly recorded aboard USS Jackson in February 2023 after crew members observed multiple Tic Tac-shaped objects, including one said to emerge from the ocean.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #73 — USS Jackson Eyewitness Alex Wiggins
entities.summary: Active-duty Navy service member Alex Wiggins describes witnessing a glowing Tic Tac-shaped object emerge from the ocean near USS Jackson and join three similar objects before departing in formation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #74 — Matthew Brown and Immaculate Constellation — Part 1
entities.summary: Matthew Brown publicly identifies himself as the author of the Immaculate Constellation report and explains his background, how he encountered the term, and why he chose to come forward.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #74 — Matthew Brown Part 1: Immaculate Constellation
entities.summary: Matthew Brown joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp for the first part of a discussion concerning his allegations about Immaculate Constellation, compartmentalized UAP programs, whistleblower risk, and congressional oversight.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #75 — Matthew Brown and Immaculate Constellation — Part 2
entities.summary: Matthew Brown discusses documents he says he reviewed, the reporting process he followed, and institutional barriers he encountered before publicly identifying himself.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #75 — Matthew Brown Part 2: Immaculate Constellation
entities.summary: In Part 2, Matthew Brown joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp to discuss documents he says he reviewed, the alleged scope and compartmentalization of Immaculate Constellation, and the reporting process he followed before identifying himself publicly.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #76 — Matthew Brown and Immaculate Constellation — Part 3
entities.summary: In the conclusion of the interview, Matthew Brown discusses alleged classified UAP imagery, secrecy mechanisms, public perception management, and his appeal for other insiders to come forward.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #76 — Matthew Brown Part 3: Immaculate Constellation
entities.summary: In Part 3, Matthew Brown concludes his WEAPONIZED interview with Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp by discussing alleged UAP imagery found on classified systems, the management of public perception, excessive secrecy, congressional inquiry, and his call for additional insiders to come forward.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #77 — UAP Briefings, Immaculate Constellation, and the Jellyfish Debate
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review closed-door congressional briefings, questions raised by the Matthew Brown interviews, the McMinnville UFO Festival, and AARO commentary concerning the Jellyfish UAP.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #78 — Sean Kirkpatrick, AARO, and UFO Politics
entities.summary: The hosts challenge claims attributed to former AARO director Sean Kirkpatrick and discuss UFO politics and Contact in the Desert with Ernie Cline and Dan Cleary.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #79 — The Large Disc UAP Video
entities.summary: The hosts discuss renewed claims that UFO reports stem from government deception and release military-sensor video described as showing a large disc-shaped UAP moving through cloud cover over a conflict zone.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #8 — Baghdad Phantom Analysis and the UFO “Silver Bullet”
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp respond to analyses of the Baghdad Phantom, discuss new comments from Jay Stratton and Travis Taylor about the 2019 Navy swarms, and renew questions about alleged reverse-engineering programs.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #80 — UFOs, Nuclear Weapons, and the Wall Street Journal
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp, joined by Robert Hastings and Marik von Rennenkampff, rebut Wall Street Journal reporting about UFO history and examine longstanding reports of UAP activity near nuclear weapons sites.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #81 — The Full Mosul Orb Video
entities.summary: WEAPONIZED releases the full four-second Mosul Orb video and interviews journalist Dustin Slaughter about obtaining the footage through government-records efforts.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #82 — Disclosure, Drones, and Competition for Crash Materials
entities.summary: The hosts survey recent UAP disclosure developments, including statements by administration insiders, additional military and intelligence witnesses, congressional interest in secret programs and drones, and reported competition for access to alleged crash materials.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #83 — Storming the Silence — Dave Foley and the Fight for UAP Transparency
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp interview comedian and actor Dave Foley about his personal UFO sighting, his changing views, and the broader struggle over disinformation, whistleblower suppression, and media treatment of UAP.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #84 — False Prophets and Flying Objects — The Whistleblower Wave
entities.summary: The hosts examine preparations for a September congressional UAP hearing, the reluctance of firsthand witnesses to testify, competing pressure from transparency advocates and secrecy interests, and a newly surfaced Defense Department case discussed with Dustin Slaughter.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #85 — Weaponized Silence — The UFO Disclosure Dilemma
entities.summary: Joe Murgia, Danny Silva, and Ryan Robbins join the hosts to assess the approaching congressional hearing, witness intimidation, definitions of disclosure, disinformation, and the obstacles facing efforts to obtain undeniable public evidence.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #86 — Threats, Intimidation, Prosecution — The Daunting Risks for UFO Whistleblowers
entities.summary: Attorney Richard Beckwith and aerospace executive Mike Gold explain the legal, professional, and practical risks facing cleared personnel who consider disclosing sensitive UAP information to Congress, including confusion surrounding promised classified briefings.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #87 — Alien Bodies and Technology — The Lost Tapes of UFO Whistleblower Col. Corso
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp revisit Philip J. Corso’s claims about Roswell materials, Pentagon technology transfer, and reverse engineering through interviews recorded before publication of The Day After Roswell.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #88 — Injured by UFOs — The Case-by-Case Medical Evidence
entities.summary: Physicist John Priestland discusses the Unhidden Foundation’s review of physical, physiological, and psychological injuries reported after close encounters and calls for release of AAWSAP records relevant to medical and scientific study.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #89 — The 2025 UAP Hearing — Did It Move the Needle?
entities.summary: Participants in the September 2025 congressional UAP hearing discuss preparation, testimony, public reaction, whistleblower risks, and military video showing a missile engagement with an unidentified object over Yemen.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #9 — Harry Reid’s AAWSAP Briefing and the Hidden History of AATIP
entities.summary: The hosts recount Harry Reid’s March 2018 closed-door meeting connecting George Knapp with AAWSAP manager James Lacatski and explain distinctions between AAWSAP and the publicly reported AATIP effort.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #90 — The Reluctant UFO Whistleblower — Dylan Borland Tells All: Part 1
entities.summary: Dylan Borland recounts his path from the U.S. Air Force into classified contractor work, an alleged firsthand encounter with UAP technology, and claimed threats, clearance manipulation, intimidation, and economic pressure.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #91 — Dylan Borland Unloads — The Truth About Legacy UFO Programs: Part 2
entities.summary: In the second part of the Dylan Borland interview, Borland expands on his allegations concerning legacy UAP programs, compartmented access, intimidation, and the nature of the technology and activity he says he encountered.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #92 — The UAP Witness That Didn’t Testify — Congress Wanted You to Hear This
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp interview a military veteran who had been expected to testify before Congress and who reports seeing a large craft hovering above a runway at Eglin Air Force Base before departing at extraordinary speed.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #93 — Mystery Drones, Eglin’s Black Triangle, and Chris Sharp
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp speak with Liberation Times editor Chris Sharp about the mystery-drone wave and revisit additional details concerning the large black triangle reported over an Eglin Air Force Base runway.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #94 — Is UFO Disclosure Dead?
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp assess claims that UAP disclosure has stalled, reviewing political resistance, media narratives, and the continuing evidence that keeps transparency efforts active.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #95 — The Area 51 Crash Retrieval and Its Cover Story
entities.summary: The hosts examine a September 2025 crash near Area 51, the rapid military response, the cordoning of public land, and civilian research challenging the Air Force explanation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #96 — James Lacatski on AAWSAP, Kona Blue, and a Recovered Craft — Part 1
entities.summary: Former DIA official James Lacatski discusses AAWSAP, Kona Blue, AARO, alleged counterintelligence activity, and his authorized account of a recovered craft whose hull was reportedly breached.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #97 — James Lacatski on Disclosure, Hitchhikers, and the Tic Tac — Part 2
entities.summary: In the second part of the interview, James Lacatski discusses obstacles to disclosure, efforts to discredit official investigations, anomalous encounters, crash-retrieval questions, and whether Tic Tac objects could be U.S. technology.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #98 — Reassessing the James Lacatski Revelations
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review the implications of their Lacatski interviews, including AAWSAP, counterintelligence, AARO, recovered-craft claims, congressional access, and Harry Reid’s role.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #99 — Is This a Pivotal Moment for UFO Disclosure?
entities.summary: Ryan Robbins, Danny Silva, and Joe Murgia join the hosts to assess renewed public attention driven by The Age of Disclosure, David Grusch’s Fox interview, and James Lacatski’s AAWSAP revelations.

entities.name: Engaging the Phenomenon
entities.summary: James Iandoli explores UAP, consciousness, contact experiences, field research, and the human implications of anomalous phenomena.

entities.name: Merged
entities.summary: Ryan Graves hosts pilots, scientists, researchers, and innovators for evidence-focused discussion of UAP, aviation safety, and scientific inquiry.

entities.name: Need to Know
entities.summary: Journalistic UAP podcast combining current reporting, analysis, historical context, and disclosure discussion.

entities.name: Somewhere in the Skies
entities.summary: Ryan Sprague hosts a long-running podcast centered on UFO reports, witness experiences, investigations, researchers, and the cultural history of the phenomenon.

entities.name: That UFO Podcast
entities.summary: Andy McGrillen presents UAP news, interviews, listener discussions, and deep dives into UFOs and connected phenomena.

entities.name: The Black Vault Radio
entities.summary: John Greenewald Jr. uses declassified records and Freedom of Information Act research as the basis for interviews about government, military, intelligence, and UAP history.

entities.name: Theories of Everything
entities.summary: Curt Jaimungal hosts technically detailed long-form conversations spanning theoretical physics, consciousness, artificial intelligence, philosophy, and UAP research.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED
entities.summary: Investigative series in which Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp explore UAP, government secrecy, unusual events, documents, and firsthand testimony.

entities.name: Anatomy of a Phenomenon
entities.summary: Jacques Vallée published an early scientific appraisal of UFO reports, helping establish his long-running role in evidence-oriented UAP research.

entities.name: Gaia
entities.summary: Subscription streaming platform publishing programs on spirituality, alternative history, and disclosure topics.

entities.name: Lex Fridman Podcast
entities.summary: Long-form interview podcast

entities.name: Los Alamos Monitor
entities.summary: New Mexico newspaper that published a June 27, 1982 feature about Bob Lazar’s jet-powered Honda and described him as a physicist at the Los Alamos Meson Physics Facility.

entities.name: Luis Elizondo Publishes Imminent
entities.summary: Luis Elizondo’s memoir Imminent: Inside the Pentagon’s Hunt for UFOs was published, presenting his account of government UAP investigation and his arguments for greater transparency and oversight.

entities.name: NewsNation
entities.summary: U.S. television news network and publisher

entities.name: Passport to Magonia
entities.summary: Jacques Vallée published Passport to Magonia, advancing the influential argument that reported UFO encounters should be compared with historical folklore and broader human experience rather than interpreted only as extraterrestrial visitation.

entities.name: The Edge of Reality
entities.summary: Jacques Vallée and J. Allen Hynek published The Edge of Reality, presenting a collaborative scientific discussion of unresolved UFO evidence and investigative methods.

entities.name: The New York Times Publishes “Glowing Auras and ‘Black Money’”
entities.summary: The December 2017 New York Times investigation brought broad public attention to the Pentagon’s UAP program and the 2004 Nimitz encounter, including David Fravor’s account.

entities.name: The New York Times Publishes “‘Wow, What Is That?’”
entities.summary: The May 2019 New York Times report presented Ryan Graves’s account of recurring East Coast UAP encounters and framed them as an aviation-safety and national-security concern.

entities.name: AARO Launches a Secure UAP Reporting Mechanism
entities.summary: AARO launched a secure reporting mechanism for current and former U.S. government personnel to report direct knowledge of UAP-related programs or activities, a significant expansion of the office’s information-gathering role under Sean Kirkpatrick.

entities.name: Americans for Safe Aerospace Launches as a 501(c)(3)
entities.summary: Ryan Graves announced the launch of Americans for Safe Aerospace as a pilot-led nonprofit focused on UAP reporting, aviation safety, and transparency.

entities.name: Avi Loeb Co-Founds the Galileo Project for Systematic UAP Research
entities.summary: Avi Loeb and Frank Laukien founded the Harvard-hosted Galileo Project to move research on UAP and possible extraterrestrial technological artifacts toward transparent, validated, instrument-based scientific study.

entities.name: Avi Loeb Leads the IM1 Interstellar Meteor Recovery Expedition
entities.summary: Avi Loeb served as chief scientist for a June 2023 expedition that surveyed the Pacific seafloor near the calculated path of IM1 and recovered hundreds of spherules for laboratory analysis. The work broadened the Galileo Project’s investigation of possible extraterrestrial technological artifacts beyond aerial observations.

entities.name: Bob Lazar Appears Anonymously as “Dennis” on KLAS-TV
entities.summary: KLAS-TV aired George Knapp’s first interview with Bob Lazar, who appeared in silhouette under the pseudonym “Dennis” while describing alleged work at S4.

entities.name: Bob Lazar Publicly Identifies Himself in KLAS-TV Interview
entities.summary: Bob Lazar appeared on KLAS-TV under his own name and face as George Knapp continued reporting on Lazar’s allegations about S4 and Area 51.

entities.name: Bob Lazar: Area 51 & Flying Saucers Released
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell’s documentary revisiting Bob Lazar’s story was released digitally, renewing public attention around Lazar, Area 51, and S4.

entities.name: Bob Lazar’s Alleged S4 Work Period
entities.summary: Bob Lazar states that he worked at an installation called S4 near Area 51 from late 1988 into 1989, helping analyze an unconventional craft. GreyAlien records this as an attributed claim, not an established fact.

entities.name: Congress Receives the Preliminary UAP Assessment After Oversight Advocacy
entities.summary: The Office of the Director of National Intelligence delivered its preliminary UAP assessment to Congress after years of outreach and advocacy by Christopher Mellon and others seeking formal government review.

entities.name: Corbell and Knapp Publish the Jellyfish UAP Footage
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp publicly released military-filmed footage described in their reporting as the Jellyfish UAP, expanding WEAPONIZED coverage of documented UAP cases.

entities.name: David Grusch Files a PPD-19 Whistleblower Complaint
entities.summary: On May 25, 2022, David Grusch and his attorneys filed an unclassified PPD-19 intelligence-community whistleblower complaint alleging retaliation and reprisal connected to his protected UAP disclosures.

entities.name: David Grusch Provides UAP Information to the DoD Inspector General
entities.summary: In July 2021, David Grusch says he confidentially provided classified UAP-related information to the Department of Defense Inspector General concerning alleged withholding of information from Congress.

entities.name: David Grusch Serves in UAP-Related Intelligence Assignments
entities.summary: From 2019 through 2022, David Grusch served in intelligence roles that included representing the National Reconnaissance Office to the UAP Task Force and later co-leading UAP analysis at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency, according to his congressional statement.

entities.name: Disclosure Project Presents New Witness Testimony at the National Press Club
entities.summary: A public Disclosure Project event at the National Press Club featured testimony from Michael Herrera, Eric Hecker, and other witnesses making allegations about concealed UAP programs and advanced technologies. The event materially broadened public attention to both Herrera's Indonesia account and Hecker's South Pole claims; the allegations remain unverified.

entities.name: Eric Hecker Works at the Amundsen–Scott South Pole Station
entities.summary: Eric Hecker has publicly stated that he worked as a Raytheon contractor at the Amundsen–Scott South Pole Station for approximately one year beginning in November 2010. That assignment is the setting for his later allegations about IceCube and other advanced technologies; those allegations are disputed and remain unverified.

entities.name: Garry Nolan’s Genomic Study Resolves the Atacama Specimen
entities.summary: A Stanford-led genomic study involving Garry Nolan concluded that the unusual Atacama specimen was human and identified mutations associated with its skeletal abnormalities, directly addressing years of extraterrestrial speculation surrounding the remains.

entities.name: House Oversight Requests 46 Military UAP Videos
entities.summary: The House Oversight Task Force requested 46 identified military UAP videos and related records from the Department of War.

entities.name: July 2023 UAP Hearing Held
entities.summary: House Oversight subcommittee held a public UAP hearing

entities.name: Karl Nell Presents a Controlled UAP Disclosure Framework at SALT
entities.summary: Karl Nell delivered the SALT iConnections presentation “The Real Black Swan Event: The Controlled Disclosure of UAP & Non-Human Intelligence,” bringing a senior military and aerospace perspective on UAP disclosure to a major public conference.

entities.name: Karl Nell Supports the UAP Task Force and Congressional UAP Legislation
entities.summary: During the closing period of his Army career, Karl Nell served as an Army director supporting the UAP Task Force and states that his work with Defense Department and congressional leaders contributed to UAP provisions in the 2022 and 2023 National Defense Authorization Acts.

entities.name: Los Alamos Monitor Profiles Bob Lazar’s Jet Car
entities.summary: The Los Alamos Monitor published a feature about Bob Lazar’s jet-powered Honda and described him as a physicist at the Los Alamos Meson Physics Facility.

entities.name: Luis Elizondo Leaves the Pentagon and Begins Public UAP Advocacy
entities.summary: In October 2017, Luis Elizondo resigned from his Defense Department position, citing the need for greater public exposure and government cooperation in the study of UAP, and subsequently became a prominent public advocate on the issue.

entities.name: NASA UAP Independent Study Public Meeting
entities.summary: NASA held a public meeting of its UAP Independent Study Team on May 31, 2023.

entities.name: NIDS Forms a Scientific Advisory Board for Anomalous-Phenomena Research
entities.summary: The National Institute for Discovery Science assembled a multidisciplinary scientific advisory board to investigate UFO reports and other anomalous phenomena. Hal Puthoff, Eric Davis, and Jacques Vallée became part of the research network that later intersected with Bigelow-supported UAP programs.

entities.name: Puthoff and Davis Contribute Technical Research to AAWSAP
entities.summary: During the Defense Intelligence Agency-funded AAWSAP period, Hal Puthoff and Eric Davis contributed technical research on advanced aerospace concepts relevant to the government’s investigation of anomalous phenomena. Their work connected private scientific research with a formal U.S. defense program examining UAP-related questions.

entities.name: S4: The Bob Lazar Story Released Digitally
entities.summary: The documentary S4: The Bob Lazar Story was released digitally, presenting a new visual reconstruction of Lazar’s account of the alleged S4 facility.

entities.name: Sean Kirkpatrick Is Named the First Director of AARO
entities.summary: The Department of Defense announced the establishment of the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office and named Sean M. Kirkpatrick as its first director, placing him at the center of the government’s formal UAP investigation and reporting structure.

entities.name: The Galileo Project Names Luis Elizondo a Research Affiliate
entities.summary: The Galileo Project announced Luis Elizondo as a research affiliate to contribute experience in UAP investigation, site selection for UAP-Scope systems, and assessment of the societal implications of potential findings.

entities.name: The Sol Foundation Holds Its Inaugural UAP Research and Policy Symposium
entities.summary: The Sol Foundation convened its inaugural symposium at Stanford University, bringing together researchers, former officials, and policy specialists for focused discussion of UAP science, government policy, and disclosure. Karl Nell and Avi Loeb were among the listed participants.

entities.name: The Sol Foundation Holds Its Second Annual UAP Symposium
entities.summary: The Sol Foundation convened its second annual symposium in San Francisco, featuring scientific, policy, philosophical, and government-focused sessions on UAP. Garry Nolan organized and spoke at the event, while Jacques Vallée participated in a plenary discussion.

entities.name: To The Stars Academy Launches with a UAP Science and Government Team
entities.summary: To The Stars Academy of Arts & Science launched publicly with former government officials, aerospace specialists, and researchers focused on UAP, advanced aerospace concepts, and government transparency. Hal Puthoff served as co-founder and Vice President of Science and Technology.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #1 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp introduce WEAPONIZED and describe a changing public reality in which military personnel, scientists, journalists, and citizens can discuss UFO/UAP evidence more openly.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #10 published
entities.summary: Duncan Trussell joins Corbell and Knapp for a wide-ranging discussion about UFOs, artificial intelligence, spirituality, Buddhism, psychedelics, gurus, and the nature of reality.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #100 published
entities.summary: Author and filmmaker David Paulides discusses reported links among UFOs, Sasquatch, disappearances, portals, Skinwalker Ranch, and evidence presented in American Sasquatch: Man, Myth, or Monster.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #101 published
entities.summary: Representatives Anna Paulina Luna, Tim Burchett, and Eric Burlison discuss resistance to congressional oversight, blocked witness access, intimidation, the limits of public hearings, possible presidential action, and alternative strategies.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #102 published
entities.summary: Jacques Vallée joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp for a wide-ranging discussion of UFO history, Project Blue Book, remote viewing, NIDS, AAWSAP, crash retrievals, the Trinity case, congressional secrecy, science, and paths forward.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #103 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review WEAPONIZED’s 2025 reporting, including new witnesses, released UAP imagery, congressional engagement, major books and films, mystery-drone incursions, and the organized pushback against UFO transparency.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #104 published
entities.summary: Will Brown, biological anthropologist Dan Proctor, and radiologist Michele Adams examine claims surrounding three-fingered South American mummies, alleged specimen manipulation, black-market trafficking, and the evidence needed to establish authenticity.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #105 published
entities.summary: Filmmaker James Fox discusses new witnesses connected to the 1996 Varginha incident, alleged intimidation, efforts to locate medical personnel and video evidence, and a Washington press event intended to bring firsthand testimony before the public and Congress.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #106 published
entities.summary: Retired Air Force lieutenant colonel and former Thunderbird Ryan Bodenheimer describes four aerial UAP encounters, including a close call, and explains why the observed objects did not resemble conventional aerodynamic craft.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #107 published
entities.summary: George Knapp revisits his 1990s reporting from Russia and releases documents describing Soviet UFO investigations, including the SETKA collection program, Thread 3 technology analysis, pilot and satellite cases, and an alleged 1982 intrusion at a Ukrainian nuclear missile base.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #108 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell, George Knapp, and Marik von Rennenkampff analyze newly released intelligence footage from the Jordanian-Syrian border showing an object tracked by an MQ-9 Reaper and appearing to demonstrate extraordinary acceleration.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #109 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp review Bob Lazar’s surprise Austin appearance, responses to criticism of the Syria UAP footage, an unauthorized Papoose Lake intrusion, and Representative Eric Burlison’s stated plans to visit restricted facilities including Area 51.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #11 published
entities.summary: John Long, co-founder of Buddha Jones, joins Corbell and Knapp to discuss changes reshaping Hollywood, the future of theatrical and streaming entertainment, UFO-themed productions including NOPE, and the creative craft of movie-trailer marketing.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #110 published
entities.summary: Journalist Chris Sharp previews his interview with former AARO deputy director Tim Phillips, including claims that dozens of cases showed extraordinary capabilities, some UAP were detected in space, and classified videos remain unreleased.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #111 published
entities.summary: Former Air Force intelligence analyst and UAP Task Force member Lenval Logan assesses the Syria UAP footage, describes classified evidence stronger than public releases, discusses AWACS tracking of anomalous objects, and explains why original task-force personnel were excluded from AARO.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #112 published
entities.summary: Representative Eric Burlison discusses a classified facility visit, limits on what he can reveal, the credibility of whistleblowers, congressional efforts to obtain concealed UAP records, and his evolving view after reviewing sensitive information firsthand.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #115 Revisits the Bob Lazar Story
entities.summary: WEAPONIZED published Episode #115, featuring Luigi Venditelli in a discussion with Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp about S4: The Bob Lazar Story and the continuing debate surrounding Lazar’s claims.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #12 published
entities.summary: In the second half of the Colm Kelleher interview, the former AAWSAP project manager discusses anomalous effects associated with Skinwalker Ranch, the program’s technology objectives, and his stated knowledge of UFO crash-retrieval efforts.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #13 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp examine George Knapp’s post-Soviet reporting on Russian military and scientific UFO investigations, including interviews with officials and researchers connected to a decade-long nationwide Ministry of Defense program.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #14 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp review Sean Kirkpatrick’s public testimony before a Senate subcommittee chaired by Kirsten Gillibrand, including AARO’s evidence claims and newly presented Middle East UAP footage.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #15 published
entities.summary: Actor and comedian Dave Foley discusses his career, his longstanding interest in UFOs, the Storm Area 51 event, conversations with Joe Rogan, and an unexpected UFO encounter in Southern California.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #16 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp revisit covert and semi-covert UFO research conducted by Soviet scientists and academic groups, drawing on interviews George Knapp recorded during reporting trips to Russia in the 1990s.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #17 published
entities.summary: Robbie Williams discusses his career, personal challenges, media scrutiny, unusual experiences, a visit to Skinwalker Ranch, and future creative projects.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #18 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp present videos, photographs, and witness accounts from Marines who reported a large triangular object over the Twentynine Palms training base in April 2021.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #19 published
entities.summary: Following the initial Twentynine Palms release, Corbell and Knapp provide additional Marine interviews, respond to flare explanations, and explain why they consider the Mojave Triangle incident an open investigation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #2 published
entities.summary: Navy commander John “Guts” Gutierrez discusses his aviation background and the 2019 West Coast incidents in which multiple Navy warships reported sustained incursions by numerous unidentified objects.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #20 published
entities.summary: Ben Schneider of Lord Huron discusses the band’s exploration of death, time, folklore, ghosts, cosmology, and lost love, along with the musical influences and creative environment behind its work.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #22 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine Canadian MP Larry Maguire’s letter alleging that Five Eyes nations have coordinated programs to analyze and reverse-engineer recovered UAP materials, including material reportedly held by Defence Research and Development Canada.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #23 published
entities.summary: Author Whitley Strieber discusses his decades of reported encounters with non-human intelligences, the cultural impact of Communion, evolving ideas about the Visitors, crash-retrieval allegations, whistleblowers, and whether society is prepared for disclosure.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #24 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review expanding U.S. and Canadian legislative efforts to uncover classified UAP records, alleged recovered hardware, special-access programs, and contractor-held materials while assessing institutional barriers to disclosure.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #25 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review the history of congressional UFO hearings and interview Representative Tim Burchett about the anticipated July 2023 House hearing, prospective witnesses, bipartisan interest, and alleged hidden crash-retrieval materials.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #26 published
entities.summary: British journalist Chris Sharp joins Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp to examine Senator Chuck Schumer’s proposed UAP disclosure legislation, its focus on government and contractor-held records and materials, Harry Reid’s influence, and the approaching House hearing.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #27 published
entities.summary: Ahead of the July 2023 House hearing, Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp explain why the testimony of David Grusch, David Fravor, and Ryan Graves should be considered historic and revisit the Nimitz Tic Tac encounter through a key eyewitness account.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #29 published
entities.summary: WEAPONIZED revisits the 2004 Nimitz Carrier Strike Group encounters, Commander David Fravor’s pursuit of the Tic Tac object, the delayed official investigation, and why his testimony became central to modern demands for UAP transparency.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #3 published
entities.summary: Former intelligence officer Jay Stratton discusses his roles across AAWSAP, AATIP, and the UAP Task Force, including early work on the Tic Tac case and classified briefings delivered across government.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #30 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp examine efforts to discredit David Grusch after his public testimony, including reporting about his prior PTSD treatment, and discuss retaliation, whistleblower protections, and the potential chilling effect on other witnesses.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #31 published
entities.summary: Animal-welfare advocate Wayne Pacelle discusses suffering in industrial agriculture, the effects of factory-farming business models on animals and people, state-level reforms, and efforts by large agricultural interests to override those protections.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #4 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp examine the Chinese surveillance balloon and three additional objects shot down over North America, with Dave Foley joining a discussion about transparency, confusion, and links to the 2019 UAP swarms.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #5 published
entities.summary: Biochemist and AAWSAP program manager Colm Kelleher discusses his scientific background, NIDS anomaly investigations, government-funded UAP research, and the study of unusual physical and biological effects.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #6 published
entities.summary: George Knapp and Jeremy Corbell revisit a former defense-industry executive’s account involving a recovered craft from New Mexico and an allegedly living occupant, including a promised recorded deposition.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #7 published
entities.summary: The hosts examine the cultural impact and legal aftermath of the Storm Area 51 phenomenon and release multiple images of an object recorded by a U.S. Air Force Reaper drone and described as a phantom.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #8 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp respond to analyses of the Baghdad Phantom, discuss new comments from Jay Stratton and Travis Taylor about the 2019 Navy swarms, and renew questions about alleged reverse-engineering programs.

entities.summary: WEAPONIZED Episode #89, The 2025 UAP Hearing — Did It Move the Needle?, was published.

entities.summary: The hosts recount Harry Reid’s March 2018 closed-door meeting connecting George Knapp with AAWSAP manager James Lacatski and explain distinctions between AAWSAP and the publicly reported AATIP effort.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #90 published
entities.summary: WEAPONIZED Episode #90, The Reluctant UFO Whistleblower — Dylan Borland Tells All: Part 1, was published.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #91 published
entities.summary: WEAPONIZED Episode #91, Dylan Borland Unloads — The Truth About Legacy UFO Programs: Part 2, was published.

entities.summary: WEAPONIZED Episode #92, The UAP Witness That Didn’t Testify — Congress Wanted You to Hear This, was published.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #93 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp speak with Liberation Times editor Chris Sharp about the mystery-drone wave and revisit additional details concerning the large black triangle reported over an Eglin Air Force Base runway.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #94 published
entities.summary: Corbell and Knapp assess claims that UAP disclosure has stalled, reviewing political resistance, media narratives, and the continuing evidence that keeps transparency efforts active.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #95 published
entities.summary: The hosts examine a September 2025 crash near Area 51, the rapid military response, the cordoning of public land, and civilian research challenging the Air Force explanation.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #96 published
entities.summary: Former DIA official James Lacatski discusses AAWSAP, Kona Blue, AARO, alleged counterintelligence activity, and his authorized account of a recovered craft whose hull was reportedly breached.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #97 published
entities.summary: In the second part of the interview, James Lacatski discusses obstacles to disclosure, efforts to discredit official investigations, anomalous encounters, crash-retrieval questions, and whether Tic Tac objects could be U.S. technology.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #98 published
entities.summary: Jeremy Corbell and George Knapp review the implications of their Lacatski interviews, including AAWSAP, counterintelligence, AARO, recovered-craft claims, congressional access, and Harry Reid’s role.

entities.name: WEAPONIZED Episode #99 published
entities.summary: Ryan Robbins, Danny Silva, and Joe Murgia join the hosts to assess renewed public attention driven by The Age of Disclosure, David Grusch’s Fox interview, and James Lacatski’s AAWSAP revelations.
